macro_markets.query: What are the most important macroeconomic and stock market news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include major index movements, Fed/central bank news, and significant company earnings or announcements.

crypto_news.query: What are the most significant cryptocurrency and blockchain news from the past 24 hours? Include major price movements, regulatory news, and notable project developments.

items.title: Crypto policy: U.S. lawmakers move forward with housing bill that includes CBDC ban
items.summary: U.S. House and Senate leaders reached a deal to advance a housing bill that would prohibit the Federal Reserve from creating a central bank digital currency through 2030. The latest version keeps the CBDC ban language that had already passed the Senate in March.

items.title: Political spending: Crypto PAC backs Alabama runoff with multimillion-dollar ad campaign
items.summary: The crypto industry-backed PAC Defend American Jobs reported spending more than $4.7 million on media and ads to support Republican Barry Moore in Alabama's Senate runoff, bringing its total related spending to more than $12 million. The activity highlights continued cryptocurrency-sector involvement in U.S. elections.

items.title: Robinhood cuts 10% of workforce amid restructuring
items.summary: Robinhood said it is reducing 10% of its full-time employees as part of an effort to flatten its organizational structure and improve efficiency. The company framed the move as a restructuring step rather than a response to weakening business conditions.

ai_news.query: What are the most important artificial intelligence and machine learning news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include new model releases, company announcements, research breakthroughs, and industry trends.

gaming.query: What are the most notable video game and gaming industry news from the past 24 hours? Include game releases, announcements, esports, and industry developments.

tech_news.query: What are the most important technology news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include major product launches, company news, and significant tech industry developments. Exclude AI-specific news.

items.title: Google releases Android 17 and Wear OS 7
items.summary: Google has started rolling out Android 17 and Wear OS 7, with support first arriving on Pixel devices. The update adds a new bubble-style multitasking UI and live updates on Wear OS.

items.title: Snap unveils $2,195 standalone Specs AR glasses
items.summary: Snap introduced its first fully standalone AR glasses, called Specs, priced at $2,195. The company says preorders are open now and shipping is planned for this fall in the U.S., U.K., and France.

items.title: Qualcomm launches Snapdragon Reality Elite for next-gen AR headsets
items.summary: Qualcomm announced a new Snapdragon Reality Elite chip aimed at boosting performance for AR and mixed-reality devices. The launch suggests continued investment in next-generation wearable computing hardware.

items.title: Xreal reveals Android XR Aura glasses
items.summary: Xreal officially revealed its Android XR Aura glasses and is taking reservations ahead of a planned launch later this year. The company did not announce a final price at the time of the reveal.

items.title: Dell XPS 13 goes on sale starting at $599
items.summary: Dell’s XPS 13 laptop is now available to buy, with pricing starting at $599. The launch expands the company’s mainstream laptop lineup with a new entry-level option.

items.title: Italy probes Apple’s iCloud services
items.summary: Italian regulators have opened a probe into Apple over iCloud services, including features such as iPhone backups. The investigation adds to regulatory scrutiny of major cloud and mobile ecosystem services in Europe.

items.title: India temporarily blocks Telegram
items.summary: India has temporarily blocked Telegram, saying the move is intended to prevent cheating during a major entrance exam. The restriction highlights continued government action against messaging platforms during sensitive periods.
